metrics.narrative: Sidel articulated a clear operational pain on the 26 Jun discovery call: manual RFQ workflows for direct materials (highly-engineered machined parts) that require technical drawing and specification ingestion to auto-classify and route to qualified suppliers. Sergio Moreira (Project Manager Supply Chain Digitization) described the current manual process and the need for autonomous negotiation on these parts. However, no quantified business case was provided—no cycle-time reduction target, cost-per-RFQ, error rate, or headcount savings. Julie Papier (Sourcing Director Indirect and Services) and Ignacio Arenas (Indirect Procurement) confirmed the pain but did not quantify impact. The qualitative need is confirmed; building a quantified ROI case is the next step to anchor the business case and unlock budget/exec sponsorship.

champion.narrative: No champion has emerged yet. The 26 Jun discovery call engaged four operational stakeholders: Sergio Moreira (Project Manager Supply Chain Digitization), Julie Papier (Sourcing Director Indirect and Services), Ignacio Arenas (Indirect Procurement), and Gilles Cloarec (IT Manager). Julie is listed as the sole Salesforce contact role (Evaluator), indicating she is the primary contact, but she has not demonstrated advocacy (no introductions, no internal docs shared, no competitive defense). The call was collaborative and exploratory, not champion-building. Sergio is the direct-materials pain owner and the most engaged on the 26 Jun call, but his access to the economic buyer and willingness to advocate are unconfirmed. The deal is too early (1 day in Qualified) to expect a champion, but building one—likely Sergio or Julie—is a continuous priority starting with the demo. The move is to ask during the demo who internally is driving the AI/automation initiative and who can champion Zycus to the exec sponsor.

competition.narrative: Sidel is competing Zycus against two alternatives: (1) SAP Ariba, which is on their future roadmap ('in the pipe to use it') for RFQ workflows but not currently deployed, and (2) the manual process (do-nothing). The buyer confirmed 'we are exploring several tools right now' on the 26 Jun call, indicating a multi-vendor exploration, but no other vendors (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) were named. SAP Ariba is the primary competitive threat: if Zycus does not lock a PoC quickly, Ariba's RFQ module activation could block the deal. The wedge is Agentic AI and Merlin autonomous negotiation, which Ariba does not offer. The activation is to make the Merlin negotiation agent the Phase 2 centerpiece and demonstrate drawing-ingestion capability (if confirmed by Pierre's 30 Jun validation) as a differentiation point. The how-we-win is speed-to-PoC and autonomous-negotiation depth.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expects two core capabilities: (1) autonomous RFQ automation with technical drawing/spec ingestion for direct materials (highly-engineered machined parts), and (2) Agentic AI (Merlin autonomous negotiation). Zycus fit for (1) is UNCONFIRMED—Pierre acknowledged 'most of our use cases today are more on indirect procurement' and committed to validate by 30 Jun (today). Fit for (2) is aligned (Merlin is in scope). If drawing ingestion cannot be supported, the core requirement is unmet and deal fails. ERP integration breadth (12 systems, gradual S/4HANA migration) could surface as complexity during PoC.

hard.next_step: Execute NDA, followed by a detailed solution walkthrough focused on Agentic AI and RFQ automation to jointly identify priority use cases and define areas where Zycus can add value.

This is the single highest-leverage action this week.

Gilles Cloarec (VP Strategic Sourcing, account contact) is the obvious next thread. During the demo follow-up this week, ask Julie/Sergio who approves the transformation budget and commercial contract, and request an intro to that person for the customized demo. An enterprise deal with a 12–15 month cycle and 20+ FTE cost-reduction business case will require exec sign-off; map the EB now while momentum is live, not after the demo when the eval slows.
